ITitleXHV::MicrophoneRawDataReady

Title-defined callback called by the XHV engine to submit raw microphone (voice) data to the title before that data is encoded by the engine. This function is called at DPC level (see Kernel DPC Callbacks).

HRESULT MicrophoneRawDataReady(
    DWORD dwLocalPort,
    DWORD dwSize,
    VOID *pData,
    BOOL *pVoiceDetected
);

Parameters

dwLocalPort
[in] Port of the local talker associated with the microphone data callback.
dwSize
[in] Size of raw microphone data.
pData
[in] Pointer to raw microphone data.
pVoiceDetected
[out] Pointer to a variable that receives a BOOL indicating whether the title detected voice in the microphone data. Ignored unless the title has enabled custom VAD detection (see Remarks below).

Return Values

Returns S_OK if successful, an error code otherwise. A title can return E_NOTIMPL if it does not implement MicrophoneRawDataReady.

Remarks

Titles use this callback to process voice data from the microphone before that data is encoded and processed by the XHV engine. The raw data can be modified by the title as desired (to add voice effects, for example) before returning from the callback function. This function is called at DPC level, so the normal restrictions for executing at that level apply.

The pVoiceDetected parameter is used by the title to return a custom voice activation detection (VAD) result to the engine to indicate whether voice was detected in the packet. This custom VAD result is used only if the bCustomVADProvided member of the XHV_RUNTIME_PARAMS structure was set to TRUE when XHVEngineCreate was called. Otherwise, the engine performs its own VAD pass. If the title has enabled custom VAD detection, it cannot return E_NOTIMPL for MicrophoneRawDataReady.

The following constants, defined in xhv.h, describe the format of raw microphone data.

Value Description
XHV_VOICE_FRAMETIME Frame time, in milliseconds.
XHV_SAMPLE_RATE Sample rate, in Hz.
XHV_BYTES_PER_SAMPLE Bytes per sample.
XHV_NUMBER_OF_SAMPLES Number of samples in a frame (XHV_VOICE_FRAMETIME * XHV_SAMPLE_RATE / 1000)
XHV_PCM_FRAMELENGTH Length of a frame, in bytes (XHV_NUMBER_OF_SAMPLES * XHV_BYTES_PER_SAMPLE)

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xhv.h.

Import Library: Use Xvoice.lib.

See Also

Kernel DPC Callbacks | IXHVEngine::SetCallbackInterface, XHVEngineCreate, XHV_RUNTIME_PARAMS
